sutta: on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was staying near raja gaha in the bamboo grove at the squirrels feeding ground now on that occasion vasakara the brahmin a great official of magadha came to visit the exalted 1 and on coming to him greeted him courteously and after the exchange of greetings and courtesy sat down at 1 side as he said thus asakara the brahmin said this to the exalted 1 master gotama we brahmins proclaim a man if he possesses 4 qualities as 1 of great wisdom as a great man what are the 4 qualities here in master gautama he is learning of whatsoever he hears he understands the meaning as soon as it is uttered saying this is the meaning of that saying this is the meaning of that saying moreover he has a good memory he can remember and recall a thing done long ago said long ago again in all the business of a householder he is killed and diligent and therein he is resourceful and capable of investigating what is proper to be done what should be arranged now master gotama if a man possesses these qualities we proclaim him as 1 of great wisdom as a great man if the worthy gotama thinks me worthy of commendation hearing let him command me on the contrary if he thinks me blame worthy let him blame me therefore i'll just stop here for a while this brahmin is the green official of uh magda and he addresses our buddha as master gotama because gotama is the surname of our buddha and sometimes our buddha is addressed as samana gotama samana is a recluse renuncian so these 4 qualities which the brahmin says is all marks of a great man great man with a great wisdom the first 1 he is learned presumably he's learned in the brahmin scriptures and worldly knowledge etc the second 1 uh he understands uh whatever he hears he understands that's the second 1 the third thing he has a very good memory the fourth 1 in the business of a householder is killed in the in the in the business of a householder and these things he thinks would make a great man then the buddha said well brahman and neither commend you nor blame you hearing i myself proclaim a man possessed of 4 qualities to be 1 of great wisdom to be a great man what are the 4 here in brahmin we have a man given up to the welfare of many folk to the happiness of manifold by him are many folk established in the aryan knowledge to it in what is of a lovely nature in 1 is of a profitable nature [Music] then the second 1 to whatsoever train of thought he wishes to apply himself to that train of thought he applies himself to whatever train of thought he desires not to apply himself to that train of thought he applies not himself whatever intention he wishes to intend he does so or not if he so wishes thus he is master of the mind in the ways of thought and the third 1 also he is 1 who attains at will without difficulty and without trouble the 4 janas which belong to the higher mind which even in this very life are blissful to abide in and the fourth also by destruction of the asavas in this very life thoroughly comprehending it of himself he realizes the liberation by mind the liberation by wisdom and attaining it abides therein no brahman i neither command or blame you hearing but i myself proclaim a man possess of these 4 qualities to be 1 of great wisdom to be a great man these 4 qualities that the buddha mention the first 1 is he he is always looking after the welfare of many foreign many people he cares for the welfare of others the second 1 is um he's um he's a master of his mind to whatever train of thought he wishes to imply himself he can do so whatever train of thought he does not wish to apply himself and also he can do so then the third 1 he attains the 4 janas the fourth 1 he has destroyed the ah savasana now like to mention again the first 1 is this person he is always concerned with the welfare of others but then but in the in the in the sense that he establishes people in the aryan knowledge that means he teaches the aryan dharma to others this is what is meant by helping others now in the uh buddha's teaching the buddha said of all the gifts that we can make to others the highest gift is the gift of the dhamma that she's teaching of the truth that is real compassion 2 worldly people like doing charity and doing uh good deeds becoming a vegetarian and all that and that is good and it is it is good but then of all these actions and the highest is the gift of knowledge because if we think about it carefully suppose somebody is in difficulty and comes to us needs help for example a person is hungry so if you are concerned with that person's welfare he will give him food to eat you feed him but after a few hours he's hungry again then you have to feed him again so tomorrow he's hungry again so are you going to feed him the whole life if you can do it it's very good but then it will be more skillful of you if you can teach him for example a trait a skill a work so if he teaches if he is taught a workman which he can do to earn a living then you have no need to look after him day after day that he will be able to take care of himself and succeed in the world but then that is just this lifetime he can earn a living but then the next lifetime when he comes he might suffer because he doesn't know how to walk the good path and the path that cult there where we cultivate a lot of blessings and to help us and then [Music] because he has still continues to be selfish et cetera next life when he comes back now he'll suffer again and not be able to get food again but if we teach him the dharma and then we teach him make him understand that all these karmic obstructions that he is experiencing now is due to karma past for example he has been selfish in this life or in the past life and because of that not many people want to help him and then he understands that he has to cultivate blessings so that people can like him to speak kind words uh harsh words etc and then um slowly yeah and he's also got to understand that karma is not only past life karma but this life karma has got to exert himself to look after himself in this life also so he not only gets a job to succeed in the world he also cultivates some some of the good qualities in life now which will help him not only this life even the next life so if we teach him this kind of dharma then we are not benefiting him not only this life we are also benefit benefiting him in the next life or the next few lives to come that's why the gift of dharma is the highest gift and because when we teach dharma to people we are teaching them to lead a skillful life instead of an unskillful life the kusala and akusala skills this workforce lies can be translated as skillful or wholesome a wholesome life a skillful life because in life we find of suffering there are 2 types of suffering there's a type of suffering which nobody can avoid for example getting old growing old becoming sick and dying nobody can run away from that all beings would experience that but there are certain kinds of suffering which are unnecessary which we can avoid for example if a person drinks and drives and you drink and drive again to an accident that was not necessary it could have been avoided so in the same way there are also other things that we can cultivate now to to avoid suffering for example if we speak skillful words to people instead of harsh words and then that we can avoid unnecessary suffering in 1 way another 1 is we know how to keep our precepts and 1 if you know how to listen to the dharma if you know how to do charity etc so these are all the teachings that the dharma teaches us to lead a very skillful life so that we go through life with the minimum suffering and not only that even the minimum suffering now eventually we sort of make it dry out by getting our samsara out of the round of rebirths then the second quality here is that person is a master of his mind he is so his mind is so well developed that he does not [Music] he's not controlled by the mind he controls the mind a lot of people they they meditate and then after many years of meditation and they think they are very skillful they think they have attained great stages but then very you can see their likes and dislikes if they have very strong likes and dislikes that means they have not uh developed mine if you have a development we would not be controlled by our likes and dislikes and then the third 1 is the jhanas the condition for wisdom is the concentration and the the fourth 1 is destruction of the asavaza then the brahmin said it is wonderful master gautama it is marvelous master gautama how well this has been said by the wordy gotama i myself do hold the worthy gautama to be possessed of these same 4 qualities indeed the worthy gotama is given up to the welfare of many folk through the happiness of many folk by him are many folk established in the aryan knowledge to it in what is of a lovely nature in what is of a profitable nature indeed the worthy gautama to whatever train of thought he wishes to apply himself to that train of thought he applies himself surely the worthy gautama is master of the mind in the ways of thought surely the master the worthy gautama is 1 who attains that will the 4 jaanas surely the word worthy gautama by destruction of the ask of us realizes the liberation by mind deliberation by wisdom and attaining it abides therein and the buddha said indeed brahman your words come close and challenge me to a statement nevertheless i will satisfy you by replying i am indeed given up to the welfare of many folk to the happiness of many folk by me are many folk established in the aryan knowledge to win in what is of a lovely nature in what is of a profitable nature to whatsoever train of thought i desire to apply myself to that i apply myself or not as i please whatever intention i wish to intend i do so or not as i please indeed brahman i'm master of i am master of the mind in the ways of thought indeed i am 1 who attains that will before jana's indeed brahmin by the destruction of the assad i realize the liberation by mind and liberation by wisdom and attaining it abide therein and that's the end of the suta
commentary:
here so it's another case where somebody is challenging the buddha and he he acknowledges that he has attained all these 4 qualities
